---
title: "Component A"
---

> **Rename this page** to reflect the actual component (e.g., "MLP Encoder", "CNN Backbone", "Transformer Block").

## What — Model / Process

- **Model type**: [e.g., multi-layer perceptron / convolutional encoder / self-attention block]
- **Process**: maps raw input features → a fixed-dimension latent representation
- **Input contract**: tensor `x` of shape `[B × D_in]`, normalised to zero mean / unit variance upstream
- **Output contract**: tensor `h` of shape `[B × H]`; consumed by Component B; no side effects
- **Key property**: output is deterministic at inference (dropout disabled via `model.eval()`)

## Why — Selection Rationale

- **Primary reason**: [e.g., MLP with LayerNorm handles arbitrary batch sizes including B=1 — BatchNorm would fail]
- **Why not alternative A** *(e.g., CNN)*: input has no spatial structure; convolution inductive bias is irrelevant
- **Why not alternative B** *(e.g., Transformer)*: [e.g., dataset too small for attention to learn meaningful patterns]
- **Necessary condition**: [state when this model makes sense — e.g., "tabular features with no explicit ordering"]
- **When to reconsider**: [e.g., if input becomes sequential or spatial, switch to Transformer or CNN backbone]

```{dot}
//| label: fig-comp-a-overview
//| fig-cap: "Component A: input contract, internal structure, and selection rationale"
//| fig-width: 7
//| fig-height: 3.5
digraph G {
  bgcolor="transparent"
  rankdir=TB
  graph [ranksep=0.5, nodesep=0.55]
  node [style=filled, fillcolor="#2c3035", color="#4a9eda",
        fontcolor="#f8f9fa", fontname="Arial", fontsize=11,
        shape=box, width=1.55, height=0.42]
  edge [color="#6c757d", penwidth=1.4]

  subgraph row1 {
    rank=same
    In [label="Input  x", fillcolor="#375a7f"]
  }
  subgraph row2 {
    rank=same
    LN    [label="LayerNorm"]
    Skip  [label="skip path", style=dashed, color="#6c757d", fontcolor="#868e96"]
  }
  subgraph row3 {
    rank=same
    Lin [label="Linear  W, b"]
    Act [label="Activation"]
  }
  subgraph row4 {
    rank=same
    Out [label="Output  h", fillcolor="#1a4a3a", color="#00bc8c"]
  }

  In  -> LN  -> Lin -> Act -> Out
  In  -> Skip -> Out [style=dashed, color="#6c757d"]
  LN  -> Act
}
```

<details>
<summary><strong>How — Formulas and Internal Mechanism</strong></summary>

**Single block forward pass** (pre-norm residual):

$$h^{(l)} = h^{(l-1)} + W_2 \,\sigma\!\left(W_1 \,\text{LN}(h^{(l-1)}) + b_1\right) + b_2$$

where $l \in \{1, \ldots, L\}$, $W_1 \in \mathbb{R}^{H_\text{ff} \times H}$,
$W_2 \in \mathbb{R}^{H \times H_\text{ff}}$, and $\sigma$ is the chosen activation.

**Activation choice**:

- `GELU` preferred — smooth gradient near zero, empirically outperforms ReLU on tabular tasks
- Alternative: `SiLU` / `Swish` — similar performance, slightly higher compute cost

**LayerNorm** normalises across the feature dimension $H$ (not batch dimension):

$$\text{LN}(h) = \frac{h - \mu}{\sqrt{\sigma^2 + \epsilon}} \odot \gamma + \beta, \quad
\mu = \tfrac{1}{H}\textstyle\sum_i h_i, \quad
\sigma^2 = \tfrac{1}{H}\textstyle\sum_i (h_i - \mu)^2$$

**Dropout** applied after activation, rate $p$ set by `method.dropout`; disabled in `eval()` mode.

**Initialisation**: Kaiming uniform for $W_1$, $W_2$; zeros for biases; $\gamma=1$, $\beta=0$.

**Config keys**: `method.hidden_dim` ($H$), `method.num_layers` ($L$),
`method.dropout` ($p$), `method.ff_multiplier` ($H_\text{ff} = \text{multiplier} \times H$).

| Key | Default | Sensible range | Primary effect |
|-----|---------|---------------|----------------|
| `method.hidden_dim` | — | 64 – 512 | model capacity |
| `method.num_layers` | — | 1 – 6 | representational depth |
| `method.dropout` | — | 0.0 – 0.4 | regularisation strength |
| `method.ff_multiplier` | 4 | 2 – 8 | FFN width relative to hidden |

```bash
# Override example
python -m src.main method.hidden_dim=256 method.num_layers=3 method.dropout=0.1
```

</details>
